stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 4 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 10 observations with mean 54.7 % (within-hospital SD = 9.1). hospital B has 5 observations with mean 50.4 % (within-hospital SD = 3.7). hospital C has 10 observations with mean 51.3 % (within-hospital SD = 6.9). hospital D has 20 observations with mean 57.0 % (within-hospital SD = 7.4). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.1 and 7.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital B: 50.8 % (shrunk from raw 50.4). Range [50.6, 51.3] as between-hospital SD varies from 3.1 to 7.6. Pooled mean: 54.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 54.5 % (weighted average of 4 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=5.1: B = 0.905.
Interval [50.6, 51.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (50.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (54.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
